steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $8x \left({x}^{9}-{x}^{8}+11\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: The terms are $8x^{10}$, $-8x^9$, and $88x$. Each coefficient is divisible by $8$, and each term contains at least one factor of $x$, so the greatest common factor is $8x$.

steps.title: Write the equivalent expression
steps.content: Factoring out $8x$ gives $$8x(x^9-x^8+11)$$ This matches the correct answer.
